records.ety: [F. perscueter, L. persequi, persecutus, to pursue, prosecute; per + sequi to follow, pursue. See Per-, and Second.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Persecuted (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Persecuting.]

defs: To pursue in a manner to injure, grieve, or afflict; to beset with cruelty or malignity; to harass; especially, to afflict, harass, punish, or put to death, for adherence to a particular religious creed or mode of worship.
quotes: Do good to them that hate you, and pray for them which despitefully use you, and persecute you.

defs: To harass with importunity; to pursue with persistent solicitations; to annoy.
